summary: Soundboard capture (a little echoy) delivers a solid ’89 spring show highlighted by a bouncy China > Rider and a killer Other One.
review: The SBD recording is generally clear, with only a slight boom/echo in the venue, making the performance easy to follow. Fans remember this as an average‑to‑good Dead concert—second‑set gems like China > Rider, Masterpiece, Birdsong and a standout Other One keep it memorable, while Jerry’s guitar and vocals are praised and the drum work (Mickey & Bill) receives special kudos.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars, praising both the musical moments and the SBD sound; only one low/neutral rating appears, so the weighted average leans toward a strong positive assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear SBD source with slight echo/booming characteristic of the Coliseum space; overall crisp and listenable, no major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical spring‑89 set with a strong second half; the vibe was relaxed and communal, making it a solid, if not classic, Dead performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Several reviewers note Jerry’s guitar tone and vocals were especially good, calling him “looking and singing better than he had in a long time.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) receives mixed remarks – one reviewer isn’t a big Brent fan but acknowledges a brief moment of his best playing.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann’s cymbal synchronicity is highlighted, and the Drums>Space segment is called excellent.
